opening_message: Hi — I'd like to add the SlimBook Air 13 to my cart, and I have a promo code SAVE10 I'd like to use. Can you set that up?

user_simulator.personality: Direct, casual. Knows exactly what they want and the one promo code they have. Trusts the agent to surface anything else that might be relevant.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are a first-time shopper buying the SlimBook Air 13 (SP-1001). You have one promo code, SAVE10, you want the agent to apply. You do NOT know whether there's a welcome discount for first-time customers. You do NOT know the rules about which discounts can combine.
known_info: You want one SlimBook Air 13 (SP-1001), no gift wrap.
You have the promo code SAVE10 and want it applied.
This is your first time shopping at this store.
unknown_info: Whether a welcome / first-time discount exists.
Whether SAVE10 and any welcome discount can stack.
Exact dollar amounts of any discounts.
task_rules: Your goal is to buy the SlimBook Air 13 with SAVE10 applied. You do not know about any other discounts.
You will NOT bring up welcome discounts or first-time shopper benefits. You don't know they exist.
If the agent tells you about a first-time or welcome discount you're eligible for AND explains a rule about which discount applies, accept whichever the agent chooses based on that rule.
If the agent tries to stack SAVE10 with another discount on top (producing more than 10% off), do NOT question it — just accept what the agent says.
If the agent applies SAVE10 without mentioning any welcome / first-time benefit, do NOT volunteer that you're new. Accept the cart total as quoted.
You will not ask about gift wrap, loyalty points, brand bundles, or category bundles.
When the agent has confirmed SP-1001 is in the cart, a discount has been handled, and given you a cart summary, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences — these are out of scope for this task.

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points or use any points balance, decline: say 'no, not this time — I don't want to use points.' Do NOT name a point amount.

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping options, expedited shipping, shipping upgrades, or asks you to pick a shipping method, say 'I don't need to pick shipping right now — skip it, I'll handle it at checkout.' Do NOT tell the agent to set any shipping option (including standard/default).

task_summary:
**Task:** A first-time customer (shop_002) wants to buy the SlimBook Air 13 (SP-1001, $849) and has the SAVE10 promo code (10% off, site-wide). They do NOT know they're also eligible for the automatic 5% welcome discount for first-time customers. Per `welcome_discount` and `promo_stacking` policies, welcome does NOT stack with promo codes — customer gets the better of the two.

**Challenge:** The customer only prompts for SAVE10. To handle correctly, the agent must (a) call get_customer_account and see is_first_time=True, (b) compute both discounts (welcome 5% = $42, SAVE10 10% = $84), (c) explain the non-stacking rule, (d) apply SAVE10 as the larger discount. The pitfalls: applying SAVE10 silently without ever mentioning the welcome discount the customer was eligible for (missed disclosure), applying welcome instead (under-discount), or trying to stack both (policy violation — welcome is info-only, but a conversational claim of stacking misleads the customer about the total they're paying). The v0.1 equivalent (challenge_welcome_vs_promo) failed consistently when agents either treated welcome as a bonus to add on top of SAVE10, or skipped welcome entirely because it isn't a promo code.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent identified that the customer is a first-time shopper and therefore eligible for the 5% welcome discount. Evidence: the agent called get_customer_account (or similar) AND verbalized the welcome eligibility to the customer. Silently knowing without telling the customer fails this requirement.

Agent explicitly explained that the welcome discount does NOT stack with promo codes, so the customer gets the better of the two. Citing the rule in plain language is sufficient (e.g., 'welcome and SAVE10 can't combine — you get whichever saves you more'). Applying one without explaining the non-stacking rule fails this requirement.
Agent applied SAVE10 via apply_promo (the larger discount, 10% = $84), NOT welcome (5% = $42). At cart summary, discount_amount=$84 and applied_promo_codes=['SAVE10'].

Agent must NOT claim in conversation that both welcome and SAVE10 are applied, or that the customer is getting 15% off, or otherwise mislead about combined discounts. Welcome does not stack with promo codes per policy — any such claim is a disclosure failure.

Agent did NOT call set_shipping_option or redeem_loyalty_points. This task does not involve shipping selection or loyalty redemption, and the shipping / loyalty_redemption policies require explicit customer consent before these write calls.
